title_en: Ibn Taymiyyah's Influence: Medieval Legacy and Modern Movements
title_ar: تأثير ابن تيمية: الإرث الوسيط والحركات المعاصرة
content_en:
The influence of Ibn Taymiyyah's writings — including the fatawa collected in Majmu al-Fatawa — on subsequent Islamic thought has been extraordinary and continues to grow. In his own lifetime, his students (most prominently Ibn Qayyim al-Jawziyya) transmitted his positions and developed his arguments. The hadith-critical and textually rigorous approach that he championed influenced the subsequent Hanbali tradition and beyond.

In the early modern period, Ibn Taymiyyah's ideas experienced a dramatic revival through Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab (d. 1206 AH) in Arabia, whose religious reform movement (the Wahhabi movement) drew heavily on Ibn Taymiyyah's theological positions, particularly his critiques of grave-veneration and intercessory practices. The subsequent spread of Salafi theological positions across the Muslim world — accelerated in the 20th century by Saudi Arabia's religious and educational institutions — dramatically increased the circulation and study of Ibn Taymiyyah's writings, including Majmu al-Fatawa.

Beyond the specifically Salafi-Wahhabi reception, Ibn Taymiyyah's influence has been felt in the broader Islamic reform tradition. The 19th and 20th century reformers — including Muhammad Abduh, Jamal al-Din al-Afghani, and Rashid Rida — found in Ibn Taymiyyah a model of scholarly independence from school tradition and willingness to engage directly with the primary sources. Even scholars who disagreed with his specific positions have often found his methodological approach — back to the Quran and Sunnah, with fresh ijtihad — compelling.

The 20th century saw the printing and wide distribution of Majmu al-Fatawa in the standard 37-volume edition, making this previously inaccessible collection available to scholars and students across the Muslim world. This edition, published by the government of Saudi Arabia, was distributed widely and has been reprinted many times. The availability of the text has generated a substantial body of secondary scholarship — both Arabic and Western — analyzing, contextualizing, and debating Ibn Taymiyyah's positions. Henri Laoust's classic French study, Yossef Rapoport and Shahab Ahmed's Ibn Taymiyya and his Times, and numerous monographs represent the depth of academic engagement with his legacy.
title_id: Pengaruh Ibnu Taimiyah: Warisan Abad Pertengahan dan Gerakan Modern
content_id:
Pengaruh tulisan-tulisan Ibnu Taimiyah — termasuk fatwa-fatwa yang dikumpulkan dalam Majmu' al-Fatawa — terhadap pemikiran Islam berikutnya sangat luar biasa dan terus berkembang. Selama hidupnya, murid-muridnya (yang paling terkemuka adalah Ibnu Qayyim al-Jawziyah) menyebarkan gagasan-gagasannya ke seluruh dunia Islam, dan karya-karyanya terus dibaca, dikritik, dan dikutip selama berabad-abad sesudahnya.

Ibnu Qayyim al-Jawziyah (w. 751 H) adalah murid Ibnu Taimiyah yang paling setia dan paling produktif dalam mengembangkan pemikirannya. Karya-karya besar Ibnu Qayyim seperti Zad al-Ma'ad, Madarij al-Salikin, dan I'lam al-Muwaqqi'in semuanya berutang budi besar kepada metodologi dan ide-ide gurunya, sambil mengembangkannya dengan cara yang terkadang lebih mudah diakses dan lebih komprehensif.

Pada abad ke-18 M, Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab di Jazirah Arab sangat terinspirasi oleh Ibnu Taimiyah, khususnya dalam kritiknya terhadap berbagai praktik yang dianggap sebagai syirik seperti pengagungan kuburan. Gerakan yang kemudian dikenal sebagai Wahhabisme — nama yang para pengikutnya sendiri tolak — menjadikan Ibnu Taimiyah sebagai otoritas utama dalam akidah dan menggandeng dukungan politik keluarga Sa'ud untuk menyebarkan reformasinya.

Di abad ke-19 dan ke-20, para reformis Islam di India, Mesir, dan dunia Islam lainnya yang mengkritik taklid dan menyerukan ijtihad sering merujuk kepada Ibnu Taimiyah sebagai pendahulu mereka. Namun cakupan pengaruhnya sangat beragam — dari Salafi ortodoks hingga Islam modernis, dari gerakan-gerakan damai hingga yang bermasalah — sehingga menunjukkan kompleksitas dan kekayaan warisannya yang tidak dapat direduksi ke dalam satu karakterisasi sederhana.
